release_gate.condition: satisfied only when every classification disagreement is resolved
release_gate.reason: Classification disagreements remain unresolved; agreement percentage alone cannot satisfy the gate.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The recorded source is absent, so the primary reviewer could not verify whether the helper performs a handoff or only computes target data.
disagreements.independent_rationale: Recovered historical behavior only derives target identifiers from an in-memory event, making it a pure extraction helper rather than an observable boundary.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The named helper is absent from current source, and the remaining similarly named helper is insufficient to establish the original behavior.
disagreements.independent_rationale: Historical source shows a pure decision-reason formatter with no I/O or package handoff; it was later inlined and removed.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The exact parity-consumption symbol is absent, so its former ownership of persistence or event handoff cannot be established.
disagreements.independent_rationale: The current successor reads and removes a private parity snapshot, compares identifiers, and returns data to its caller; this is internal bookkeeping rather than an observable boundary.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The anchored property returns a constant adapter source identifier and performs no I/O, dispatch, or externally observable handoff.
disagreements.independent_rationale: The grouped finding mixes pure properties and helpers with real I/O functions, so a single record-level label cannot represent every grouped function accurately.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The function calls the external telemetry client and therefore represents a genuine emission boundary; historical source is needed to replay the original no-log state.
disagreements.independent_rationale: The complete function has logged the observation count before the external client call since introduction, so the reported absence of logging does not reproduce.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The function emits the terminal run event and records telemetry, making it a lifecycle boundary; historical source is needed to replay the reported state.
disagreements.independent_rationale: Complete current and historical implementations log and emit telemetry before the event handoff, suggesting the denial was based on incomplete source context.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The named event callback is absent from current source, and the related successor alone cannot establish the original callback behavior.
disagreements.independent_rationale: The successor filters a post-tool read event and persists recovery evidence that affects retry-lock release, making it an event-driven state transition without its own telemetry.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The centralized adapter telemetry seam is invoked by normalization and rendering paths but discards every metric, leaving observable adapter operations uninstrumented.
disagreements.independent_rationale: The reported function is the telemetry sink abstraction itself; requiring that sink to log or emit telemetry would be circular and does not identify a missing boundary log.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The recorded source is absent, so the primary reviewer could not verify whether the helper performs a handoff or only computes target data.
disagreements.independent_rationale: Recovered historical behavior only derives target identifiers from an in-memory event, making it a pure extraction helper rather than an observable boundary.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The package facade only re-exports the state mixin and contains no implementation of the named method, so the captured revision is required.
disagreements.independent_rationale: The behavior belongs to the internal recovery-state implementation, not the package facade; attributing the method to the facade is a source-location artifact.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The recovered callable constructs the cloud controller and returns its subscription stream, creating an externally observable asynchronous handoff.
disagreements.independent_rationale: The exact function body is unavailable; similar lineage contains a subscription handoff, but that does not prove the behavior of this exact draft.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The function consumes submit-attempt event details and mutates operator-visible preview state, including clearing authorization state after confirmed submission.
disagreements.independent_rationale: The function is an internal projection helper called by the actual event handler; it does not receive or dispatch the event itself, so separate boundary logging would duplicate ownership.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The recovered callable constructs the cloud controller and returns its subscription stream, creating an externally observable asynchronous handoff.
disagreements.independent_rationale: The exact function body is unavailable; similar lineage contains a subscription handoff, but that does not prove the behavior of this exact draft.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The recovered successor only conditionally calls a local widget-population helper after composition and performs no external handoff.
disagreements.independent_rationale: The exact symbol is absent from current and committed source; a later similarly placed mount callback is insufficient to prove this draft had identical behavior.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The function converts a completed postprocess result into score requests and delegates to the external telemetry emission path.
disagreements.independent_rationale: The matching source already logs the postprocess scoring boundary before delegating to the emission function, so the reported absence of logging does not reproduce.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The recorded normalization source is absent, so the primary reviewer could not prove whether the named predicate is only a pure helper.
disagreements.independent_rationale: Recovered behavior classifies dictionaries and constructs typed values in memory; it neither receives a runtime callback nor hands data to an external service.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The recorded path imports rather than defines the named function, and no historical definition at that location was found; the evaluated source snapshot is required.
disagreements.independent_rationale: The available implementation is an internal projection helper called by the actual event handler, not an independent event boundary; the path mismatch needs a fixture for exact replay.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The function calls the external telemetry client and therefore represents a genuine emission boundary; historical source is needed to replay the original no-log state.
disagreements.independent_rationale: The complete function has logged the observation count before the external client call since introduction, so the reported absence of logging does not reproduce.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The recorded metrics source is absent; the function name suggests observability but is insufficient to establish a real boundary.
disagreements.independent_rationale: Recovered historical behavior only accumulates normalized values into in-memory counters and does not emit, publish, persist, or cross a package boundary.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The anchored helper scans mapping keys, normalizes a string, and returns it; it performs no adapter, package, or event handoff.
disagreements.independent_rationale: The grouped finding combines pure parsing helpers with actual adapter entry points, so one record-level classification cannot represent the mixed behavior accurately.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The anchored property returns a constant adapter source identifier and performs no I/O, dispatch, or externally observable handoff.
disagreements.independent_rationale: The grouped finding mixes pure properties and helpers with real I/O functions, so a single record-level label cannot represent every grouped function accurately.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The class remains but the named method was replaced; historical source is needed to determine whether it owned a persistent-state handoff.
disagreements.independent_rationale: The successor validates and clears internal recovery state inside retry enforcement; it is a state-store operation whose caller owns the observable policy result.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The file now contains a differently named successor and no definition of the recorded mount callback, so the captured revision is required.
disagreements.independent_rationale: Historical source shows a post-refresh lifecycle callback that hands review state to mounted UI rendering without logging or telemetry.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The mapped source contains a differently named successor and no definition of the recorded mount callback, so historical source is required.
disagreements.independent_rationale: Historical source shows a post-refresh lifecycle callback that hands review state to mounted UI rendering without logging or telemetry.

disagreements.independent_rationale: Historical source shows a post-refresh lifecycle callback that hands review state to mounted UI rendering without logging or telemetry.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The exact parity-consumption symbol is absent and the current implementation uses a differently named successor, so the captured revision is required.
disagreements.independent_rationale: The current successor reads and removes a private parity snapshot, compares identifiers, and returns data to its caller; this is internal bookkeeping rather than an observable boundary.

disagreements.primary_rationale: The function directly calls the external telemetry client's score-creation operation and therefore crosses an emission boundary.
disagreements.independent_rationale: The matching source already logs the score-emission boundary immediately before the external client call, so the finding's no-logging premise is false.
